about.name: Zhitao Lin 林之涛
about.roles: Composer • New Media Artist • Sound Engineer
biography: Zhitao Lin is a composer and new media artist working at the intersection of sound, computation, and cultural memory. His practice combines algorithmic composition, embodied interaction, and audiovisual system design to explore how emerging technologies may open new forms of artistic creation and live performance.

Rather than approaching art as direct expression, he investigates how body, sound, and image are translated and reorganized through technological systems, and how these processes reshape perception, authorship, and aesthetic form.

Drawing on Chinese aesthetic thought, spectral thinking, and experimental performance, his work has been presented internationally, including at the International Computer Music Conference, SIGGRAPH Asia Real-Time Live, and the IRCAM Forum Workshop. He has been commissioned by the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts for a new project currently in development.
Lin is a Doctor of Musical Arts candidate in composition at the Peabody Institute of the Johns Hopkins University.

research.headline: Composer and new media artist working through sound, computation, and cultural memory.
paragraphs: My current research explores algorithmic composition, spectral listening, and the trace of gesture through live media systems.
I investigate how sonic memory becomes interface, how performance becomes archive, and how computational processes can embody cultural thought.

notes.title: Sound As System
notes.body: Composition as a set of relations between spectral material, computation, and performative decision.

notes.title: Body As Interface
notes.body: Gesture is treated as musical thought in motion, translated into sound and image through live systems.

notes.title: Memory As Form
notes.body: Cultural memory appears as resonance, trace, and transformation rather than direct representation.

projects.title: Liminal
projects.description: Gesture/body-driven AI system fusing Chinese aesthetics with real-time sound and image generation.
presentations: April 2025 — 2025 Digi Muse Music and Technology Festival, Shanghai Concert Hall, Shanghai
June 2025 — International Computer Music Conference (ICMC), Emerson College Media Art Gallery, Boston
December 2025 — SIGGRAPH Asia Real-Time Live, Hong Kong Convention and Exhibition Centre (HKCEC), Hong Kong
March 2026 — IRCAM Forum Workshops, IRCAM & Centre des Arts (CDA), Paris / Enghien-les-Bains

items.title: Bodhi 菩提
items.description: Orchestral composition with VR integration. Honorable Mention, 2024 DeLillo Composition Competition. Conducted by Ryo Hasegawa and performed by the Peabody Symphony Orchestra at Miriam A. Friedberg Concert Hall, Baltimore, April 23, 2024.

items.title: Ocean Mist at Twilight 青海波
items.description: Winner, 2022 Macht Orchestral Composition Prize. Conducted by Hae Lee and performed by the Peabody Symphony Orchestra at Miriam A. Friedberg Concert Hall, Baltimore, October 20, 2021.

items.title: Whisper of Buddha
items.description: Conducted by David Milnes and performed by the UC Berkeley Symphony Orchestra at Hertz Hall, Berkeley, April 11, 2019.

items.title: Pulsing
